features.cursorEvidence: Agents Window, unified agents sidebar, chat/cloud history and MRU switcher
features.qivrynBaseline: History view and one active chat
features.acceptance: Agents from every runtime are searchable and show consistent status on every surface; the native desktop sidebar, tabs, transcript, composer, and Agents Window pass packaged functional and visual acceptance tests.

features.cursorEvidence: Parallel local, SSH and cloud agents
features.acceptance: The GUI and CLI can launch at least four skill-backed local tasks concurrently as independently cancelable runs in isolated worktrees.

features.cursorEvidence: Background composers, durable status and reconnect
features.qivrynBaseline: BackgroundMode components are present but not integrated
features.acceptance: Runs survive UI closure and recover after process restart.

features.cursorEvidence: Nested async subagents and subagent cards
features.qivrynBaseline: CLI beta subagent tool and model role
features.acceptance: Nested tasks stream progress and can be canceled independently.

features.cursorEvidence: Queued messages and configurable queue behavior
features.acceptance: Users can add, edit, reorder, remove and run queued prompts.

features.cursorEvidence: Local-to-cloud transfer and cloud actions
features.acceptance: Runs can move between runtime adapters without transcript loss.

features.cursorEvidence: User hooks around agent operations
features.acceptance: Hooks share one schema and execute consistently across surfaces.

features.cursorEvidence: Deep links for agents and settings
features.acceptance: Stable links open runs, files, reviews, checkpoints and settings.

features.cursorEvidence: Compact composer with mode/model/context toolbar
features.qivrynBaseline: Qivryn TipTap composer with newer access selector
features.acceptance: Composer remains fully usable at 320 px without clipping.

features.cursorEvidence: Editable plans, todos and build actions
features.qivrynBaseline: Plan mode exists without Cursor-level plan artifacts
features.acceptance: Plans support edit, approve, reject, export and progress tracking.

features.cursorEvidence: Conversation checkpoints and restore
features.acceptance: Mutating batches create restorable checkpoints independent of editor undo.

features.cursorEvidence: Virtualized transcript, sticky prompts/plans and compact cards
features.qivrynBaseline: Chat transcript and collapsible tool output
features.acceptance: Ten thousand events scroll smoothly without layout shift.

features.cursorEvidence: Files, symbols, images, Git, terminal, web and MCP context
features.qivrynBaseline: Qivryn context providers, attachments, docs and MCP
features.acceptance: Main chat retains all existing context sources. Durable Agents resolve portable files, language-server symbols, terminal and Git snapshots, configured providers including web, MCP resources, and MCP prompts with bounded payloads. Direct multimodal image attachments execute locally, through read-only Docker mounts, and through temporary SSH transfer with cleanup.

features.cursorEvidence: Voice prompt input
features.acceptance: Supported platforms can record, transcribe, review and cancel voice input.

features.cursorEvidence: Chimes, native notifications and unread markers
features.acceptance: Completion notification behavior is configurable and trust-safe.

features.cursorEvidence: Inline prompt bar and full/selection edits
features.qivrynBaseline: Qivryn Edit mode and quick edit
features.acceptance: Inline edits support selection and full-file workflows with immediate cancel.

features.cursorEvidence: Syntax-aware multi-file diffs and partial accept/reject
features.acceptance: Changes can be accepted or rejected by block, file or turn.

features.cursorEvidence: Tab autocomplete with partial token/word/line acceptance
features.acceptance: Provider-independent suggestions support partial acceptance and jump navigation.

features.cursorEvidence: AI blame linked to generating chat
features.acceptance: Generated lines resolve to their originating agent event and checkpoint.

features.cursorEvidence: Incremental indexing and Cursor ignore files
features.qivrynBaseline: Qivryn local indexing and ignore handling
features.acceptance: Existing local indexing works unchanged and exposes diagnostics.

features.cursorEvidence: Semantic, lexical, symbol, Git and commit retrieval
features.qivrynBaseline: Semantic/context retrieval without full Git-history fusion
features.acceptance: Retrieval ranks code, symbols and relevant history in one budget.

features.cursorEvidence: Shadow workspace for validation
features.acceptance: Build and language checks run without mutating the active workspace.

features.cursorEvidence: Privacy controls and local-only behavior
features.acceptance: Local-only mode performs no remote retrieval or inference.

features.cursorEvidence: Worktree-isolated agent execution
features.acceptance: Every parallel local run receives an isolated dirty-tree snapshot.

features.cursorEvidence: Branch controls, retention, quotas and cleanup
features.acceptance: Users can retain, merge, export and safely clean worktrees.

features.cursorEvidence: Worktree-aware tabs, badges and tooltips
features.acceptance: Native editor tabs clearly identify repository, branch and worktree.

features.cursorEvidence: AI commit message generation
features.acceptance: One shared generator works from SCM and CLI with editable output.

features.cursorEvidence: Source-control graph integration
features.acceptance: Agent branches and checkpoints appear in the packaged IDE's native graph and are covered by Electron navigation tests.

features.cursorEvidence: Agent Review/Bugbot pane and inline findings
features.acceptance: Working tree, staged, commit, branch and file reviews share one result format and the packaged IDE opens findings in native Monaco diff and multi-diff editors.

features.cursorEvidence: Fix, reply, add-to-chat, dismiss, feedback and rerun
features.acceptance: Every native finding exposes accept, reject, comment, navigation, checkpoint restore, and rerun actions and remains anchored after edits.

features.cursorEvidence: Autofix and second review pass
features.acceptance: Autofix validates changes and reports remaining findings.

features.cursorEvidence: Native terminal prompt bar
features.acceptance: The packaged editor has a native terminal prompt bar; compatibility surfaces provide an adjacent drawer.

features.cursorEvidence: Side Chat, Second Opinion and generated commands
features.acceptance: Commands show quoting, policy, sandbox and elevation before execution.

features.cursorEvidence: Background jobs and external agent status
features.acceptance: Jobs can be stopped, reopened, observed after UI restart, and rendered as native editor inputs in the packaged IDE.

features.cursorEvidence: Native browser tabs and navigation
features.acceptance: Users can navigate local and remote sites in agent-associated native tabs with packaged browser-layout and handoff tests.

features.cursorEvidence: Screenshots, DOM, console, network, viewport and recording
features.acceptance: Browser tools share permissions, audit logs, locking and takeover.

features.cursorEvidence: Origin, certificate, dialog, download and auth controls
features.acceptance: Sensitive browser actions require explicit scoped authorization.

features.cursorEvidence: MCP OAuth, tools, prompts and resources
features.acceptance: All existing MCP capabilities remain available and share diagnostics.

features.cursorEvidence: Skills and recently-used skills
features.acceptance: Every surface discovers Qivryn, Cursor, Codex, Claude, Copilot and plugin skills; the GUI and CLI can list, create, edit and apply writable skills while preserving provenance and read-only plugin sources.

features.cursorEvidence: Project, global and generated rules
features.acceptance: Rules expose provenance, priority, read-only state and generation from selection.

features.cursorEvidence: Cursor plugins and subagent definitions
features.qivrynBaseline: Qivryn-managed local plugin registry, portable skill discovery, rules, MCP, and persisted subagents
features.acceptance: The Extensions settings surface can import or update trusted local plugin bundles, enable, disable, and uninstall them, show provenance and contribution counts, activate bundled skills, and navigate to rules, MCP/tools, and agents on VS Code and JetBrains through the shared Core protocol.

features.cursorEvidence: Cursor model picker and auto mode
features.qivrynBaseline: Qivryn arbitrary providers, roles and local models
features.acceptance: No existing provider or local model capability regresses; the last sanitized model catalog renders immediately while configuration refreshes in the background.

features.cursorEvidence: Auto-review, sandbox and allowlist controls
features.qivrynBaseline: Ask, Autonomous, Full Access and Read Only plus terminal classifier
features.acceptance: The same policy decision is shown and enforced on every surface.

features.cursorEvidence: Standalone Agents Window and title-bar controls
features.acceptance: The packaged native Agents Window supports multi-repository monitoring, shared session handoff, restoration, and full keyboard control.

features.cursorEvidence: Agent, Editor, Zen, Browser and maximized-chat layouts
features.acceptance: Built-in and saved layouts restore reliably per workspace in packaged macOS, Linux, and Windows Electron tests.

features.cursorEvidence: Cursor dark, midnight, light and high contrast
features.qivrynBaseline: VS Code theme inheritance and Qivryn theme page
features.acceptance: Qivryn-branded Dark, Midnight, Light, and High Contrast themes pass contrast checks and the CodieApp golden visual regression gates.

features.cursorEvidence: VS Code import and feature tour
features.acceptance: Packaged onboarding imports settings and teaches agents, edit, tab, privacy, skills, and MCP without showing stock Chat onboarding.

features.cursorEvidence: Native update controls and recovery dialogs
features.qivrynBaseline: Extension updates and fatal config notice
features.acceptance: Packaged update and runtime failures always provide bounded automatic retry, reload, final error, and log actions on every desktop platform.

features.cursorEvidence: Agents across remote SSH workspaces
features.qivrynBaseline: IDE remote compatibility without shared runtime adapter
features.acceptance: Remote runs reconnect without losing local metadata or events.

features.cursorEvidence: Background composer resolver and remote worker
features.acceptance: Container runs implement the same runtime contract and permissions.

features.cursorEvidence: Slack connection and thread artifacts
features.acceptance: Slack remains outside the parity target unless the target exclusions are revised.

features.cursorEvidence: NDJSON ingest service
features.acceptance: A localhost-only ingest endpoint can attach external structured events to a run.

features.cursorEvidence: Agent transcript diagnostics and runtime recovery
features.qivrynBaseline: Qivryn logs, console and fatal notices
features.acceptance: Diagnostics can export redacted runtime state without silently uploading code.

features.cursorEvidence: Automatic context compaction with a visible transcript marker
features.qivrynBaseline: Manual conversation compaction and CLI pruning
features.acceptance: Completed turns compact before the limit without dropping the active prompt. Provider token-count mismatches force one compact-and-retry cycle, oversized completed tool payloads are bounded, and local summary recovery prevents compaction failure from stopping a run.

features.cursorEvidence: Ripgrep-backed search with glob, type, context, output and pagination controls
features.acceptance: Agents can search a workspace precisely without escaping its roots or flooding context.

features.cursorEvidence: Scheduled and manually triggered background agents
features.qivrynBaseline: Hooks and ad-hoc detached local agents
features.acceptance: Local interval and manual automations persist, survive restart and start runs through the shared runtime.
